channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Schizo CCP "professor" eceleb says: >the great reset is happening soon, guys >the entire global economy is going to coll… — 47 replies — 188.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Schizo CCP "professor" eceleb says: >the great reset is happening soon, guys >the entire global economy is going to coll…

Open thread • Replies: 47 • Updated: 2026-03-31 18:07 UTC

OP

Schizo CCP "professor" eceleb says:
>the great reset is happening soon, guys
>the entire global economy is going to collapse
Is this a top or bottom signal? I'm unironically asking, this is not a troll thread.

Early replies (first 3)

62057822
>>62057776
Stupid ass right-wing grifter who knows nothing. Ignore anyone who takes this faggot seriously

62057842
>>62057776
He's a retarded leftoid grifter that thinks gay sex gives you the ability to read minds.

62057883
>>62057776
Im gonna go on an unironic whim here and say that no, the big bad collapse is not coming. I know spicy opinion, just going off of the previous gorillion year track record.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62058035
>>62057776
it's a bottom signal. reminiscent of 2012 right before the decade american bull market. they were saying all this back in 2012 about silver/oil being the future and BRICS taking over in 2 weeks so you better learn chinese. none of that happened, instead BRICS collapsed more and more and america has grown more and more.

62058074
>>62057926
anon, I...

62058075
>>62057776
Imagine listening to this Canadian leftoid CIA plant

62058085
>>62057926
face it migger you lost, to some random sandniggers out of all people lmao

62058087
>>62057998
look into the school he teaches at
>https://moonshotacademy.cn/en/
he also got kicked out of china for spying, but is back in china's but only in SARs and SEZs

there's nothing organic about this chink

62058101
>>62058075
most likely also a yale skull and bones fgt too along w/ his wife

62058151
>>62057776
>if the economy crashes every investment goes to shit
>if the economy goes to shit, cash's value plummets
what do?

62058173
>>62057776
>also, china numba 1, america just go ahead and stop trying so we can catch up

62058190
>>62058151
invest and hodl commodities duh fiat is fake and gay

62058200
>>62057918
Thanks, Captain Hindsight!

62058360
>>62058190
>implying commodities aren't turbo-speculative in 2026

62058363
>>62058151
Unironically, if everything truly crashes, the best investment would be in yourself (survival skills, personal fitness etc), your connections (friends and your personal clique that will stick with you), food storage, and guns. Lots of fucking guns and ammo.

Because it might as well be a zombie apocalypse when the world economy collapses

62058369
>>62058360
>hodl

62058376
>>62058173
That's not a chinaman, that's a manchu.

62058398
>>62057941
Professor Dave? The guy that believes men can be women? That the Covid jab was an actual vaccine? Thanks for letting us know you're a Reddit fag.

62058409
>>62057901
This must go so hard if you're retarded. He predicted a coinflip election and then predicted that the the notorious Israel simp president would do what Israel has been pushing the US to do for half a century, and that Iran would respond in the only way it was ever going to respond?

Jiang is a major IQ filter. Look up the video where he talks about poverty and scarcity. If you cannot tell this guy is full of shit your IQ is 105 in the absolute best case

62058410
>>62058085
You lost along with all other panicans
Total global economy will be fine

62058420
>>62057776
It would be a bottom signal under the current circumstances. Consider when silver peaked in February and the narrative at the time was that the Fed is collapsing and Comex will default in March. Which it didn't.

62058443
>>62058409
Post evidence you predicted all these things before the election. You won't, because you didn't and are only now pretending it was obvious.

62058519
>>62057822
He had mostly sensible takes. Every so often he's fumbled on his explanation of something but I think that may be due to a language barrier. The hate for him is pure contrarianism as typical from 4chan. He became popular, and isn't white, so you faggots MUST figure out how to dislike him.

62058524
>>62058443
Sorry but this is just more proof of the IQ filter. Even if all these things were my opinions, how would I "post evidence" of it and how would I prove it was my opinion? You are asking for something you know can't exist even if I'm right, which means you're either being dishonest or you're too dumb to understand why your question doesn't make sense.

I'm not saying it was "obvious", Trump winning was legitimately a coinflip, and the Iran war might have been an even smaller event on top of that (let's not forget Jiang predicted Haley would be the VP and that she would pressure Trump to go into Iran, which was totally wrong). But in a world with millions of grifting retards spouting random ideas where someone is bound to call it right, if you are impressed with some random high school teacher from China calling a ~10% event correctly and getting some key details wrong and think it's evidence that he's some geopolitical expert because of it, you are legitimately just not a very smart person. Again, look up the video where he talks about why poverty exists. He fundamentally does not understand the distinction between wealth and currency. His prediction for the Iran war is that the US would lose because Iran is mountainous, something that has played literally zero role in the conflict so far and never will.

If you fall for this shit and think you're dealing with an impressive expert here, you are a rube and a mark and you fall for shit easily. That's all there is to it. Do you also think the random jeet who tweeted "Donald Trump for president" in 2012 is the modern day Nostradamus? Or do you think that it might be because there's a billion people tweeting retarded thoughts all day, someone might land on Donald Trump for President?

62058554
>>62058443
If you couldn't see harris losing by a landslide you don't have a brain. That's not even being pro trump, she was just as retarded as clinton acting like the election season was a preordained inaugural party. You can't act like that when people are at their highest economic pain point in 50 years

62058587
>>62058519
You're a fucking retard and the internet needs less people like you and Jiang ruining it. There has never been an IQ filter as obvious as that stupid chink. It's not even about race because Asians are usually intelligent but this guy is a college dropout larping as a professor with dogshit opinions on everything

62058608
>>62058587
>textbook 4chan format contrarian opinion on x thing
yea ok, tell me what else can you dig up from your hivemind.

62058617
>>62058608
https://www.youtube.com/shorts/KDLXTBLmdiE
You really listen and nod along to this drivel don't you normgroid

62058633
>>62058617
Scarcity is manufactured though, beyond just money. It's the main argument against UBI and other forms of utopian ideas, which are totally possible, just deliberately not done. You think hunger is still actually an issue? That everyone can't have shelter and basic needs met in our day? (speaking mostly for the first world btw).

62058694
>>62058519
He comes across as a /pol/lok from 10 years ago that is giving lectures to community college students while unable to hide his power level. Anyone that was a regular lurker back then has the same takes. Only a brain rotted broccoli head would think anything he says is profound. If you have been on this site for years you should have the same takes as him minim the turdie F35 rage

62058716
>>62058524
>no evidence
My hero, PROFESSOR jiang, can provide archived footage of his prophecies in edutainment video form. You have succesfully exposed yourself as a fraud.

62058726
Anyone remember that ROFSCHILD thread from 2011 on GLP boomer forums?
>"the next big thing"
Turns out it was blockchain currency, coopted by epstein / Rothschild
>Libya, Lebanon, Syria
All happened as predicted
>"Iran will be last, and the fallout will usher in the new global order"
End of the fiat system starts now. Moving into digital feudalism.

https://archive.org/details/rofschildv1

62058760
>>62057941
What a uncanny valley jewish freak. I couldn't watch 10 seconds of that. I'd rather look at that pan faced chink pol schizo than that semtic creature.

item.title: I just paid around 2 Euro too much in the supermarket — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I just paid around 2 Euro too much in the supermarket

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-03-31 17:56 UTC

OP

I did not take the receipt with me and only found out calculating at home
IM ENRANGED

Early replies (first 3)

62051717
>>62051712

They stuck you for your papers in this economy?

62051726
You know what's worse that I've recently nooticed in my shithole Europoor country? It's that some large store chain kikes made the grocery bags thinner to cut corners. Faggots want the bags to fail on my brief walk to my car. Green ass niggers.

62051731
Steal something next time to balance it out

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62051746
>>62051712
always take the receipt and do a quick check before bagging your things anon , rookie mistake

62051749
>>62051731
I seriously already thought about it. Fruits should be easy?

62051751
>>62051731

They will track you and once it adds up to enough for a serious charge, they will arrest you and put you in prison.

The cashier is probably charging everyone a little more and then taking the excess from the register at the end of the shift. OP is a victim.

62051754
>>62051746
I just wanted to go away because some retarded boomer said inane things and wanted some weird thing from the cashier. Thats the reason she fucked up likely aswell.

62051776
>>62051712
>mfw zoomer cashiers don't know the difference between turnips and beetroots so I always get beetroots for the price of turnips.

62051785
>>62051776
This. It will balance out eventually. One time they didn't properly scan $40 of drinks. That was a nice surprise.

62051890
>>62051776
Lucky nigger. I only get boomer cashiers.

62052806
>>62051751
>OP is a victim.
Thank you for your recognition of the truth. This means a lot to me. This is serious matter.

62053072
>>62051712
>buy the organic product but scan as the regular
>buy the beefsteak tomoatos but scan as the roma

62058681
>>62053072
You save a lot doing it?

item.title: US here. Should I buy a house (cash) before hyperinflation kicks in ? — 30 replies — 47.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
US here. Should I buy a house (cash) before hyperinflation kicks in ?

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-03-31 17:29 UTC

OP

Utilities will probably be crazy.

Early replies (first 3)

62050666
never buy boomer bags

62050681
>>62050666
You objectively cannot argue with these digits OP.

62050682
>>62050666
what do then

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62050687
>>62050681
god damn
>>62050666
ccccc-c-c-checked

62050688
>>62050681
>trusting satan

62050749
>>62050666
FPBP
>Satanic trips don't lie

62050908
>>62050682
Hyperinflation isn't coming, economic collapse and apocalyptic deflation is

62050911
>>62050908
Nah, i think stagflation is more likely.

62050912
>>62050649
yeah dude this is the best time in history to buy a house

62050914
>>62050688
Checked.
>>62050681
He's not called the father of lies for nothing, anon.

62050944
>>62050912
In ALL of history.
>>62050649
I think like 2028-2030.

62050960
>>62050666
holy BAAAAAAAAAAASED

62051016
>>62050911
isn't it "very high" inflation instead ? both are suboptimal

62051135
>>62050649
It basically never mAkes sense to buy a house outright. Put 20% down and throw the rest of it in the stonk market

62052731
>>62051135
with these mortgage rates I will be paying 20% of what I'd pay with a mortgage if I pay outright

62052752
>>62052731
Typically he is correct. 6% interest but you should make 8% a year in s&p. But you have to pay tax on returns. But also the mortgage becomes less every year with 3% inflation. In 10 years it’s like you’re paying 75% of your mortgage and in 20 it’s like you’re paying 55% of your mortgage.

62052757
>>62051027
Are houses commodiitrs?

62052759
>>62050666
Never listen to Satan's lies.

62052765
>>62050908
>prices
>ever going down

62054026
>>62050649
if you know hyperinflation is coming then get as much debt as you can.

62054040
>>62050666
checked and correct

62054551
Realistically CreditScoreProtocol * com is the only lifeboat still available to you

62055511
>>62052752
i can deduct my property tax (and so can you if you are from the us)

62055809
>>62055511
You can also deduct mortgage interest.

You can also turn the property into a rental and deduct management fees, utilities, insurance, and any "repairs and maintenance" you want to do.

Easiest scam in the world is to rent your place out then do a bunch of renovations when a tenant moves out - you get all the renovations for 25% off.

62055886
>>62050908

noooo these deflated goods are too cheap im going insaaaanne

62056240
>>62050666
>Satan advice
I don’t know what to believe. Good or bad?

62057163
>>62050649
why dont you just ask God to give you a home instead of wasting pixels and paper?

62057169
>>62050649
Stack cash so that 300k boomer box becomes 500k when you buy and you get to pay the 500k tax rate instead. The local government thanks you for your service

62058541
>>62055809
you have to be smart about renovations since they can trigger a tax reassessment

item.title: Mid-April Will Be Wild — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Mid-April Will Be Wild

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-03-31 18:06 UTC

OP

>Iran - Israel ceasefire
>CLARITY passes
>Circle purchases Japan's US Treasuries
What a time to be alive.

Early replies (first 3)

62054995
>>62054921
itt, shit that don't matter

62055015
>>62054921
>TOAD moons

62055118
>>62054995
The only shit that doesn't matter is (You).

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62055122
>>62054921
>wild
>cooked
zoomer words. our words were better too

62055157
>>62055122
KYS generational baiter. The nigger didn’t even say cooked.

62055170
>>62054921
>Mid-April
So, literally 2 More Weeks™

62057932
Buy toad you stupid niggers.

62058586
>>62055015
2 Trillion toad hodler. I'm ready.

62058713
when should i put in my two weeks?

62058751
it sounds like the worst kind of hopium this is my second bear market 5 fucking years and i havent made it,
its just too good to be true
to think the cycle is not over
that btc will print a new ATH and alts will finally catch up
nah its a nigger life if it happens cool otherwise see you nogs in 2029

item.title: RAM scalper hate thread — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
RAM scalper hate thread

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-03-31 17:58 UTC

OP

> RAM Prices Collapse After AI-Driven Surge, Causing Micron Technology Stock Decline
> RAM prices have sharply declined following an AI-driven surge that prompted Micron Technology to exit the consumer RAM business based on unfulfilled AI commitments from OpenAI's Sam Altman. This shift led to supply shortages and elevated DDR5 prices, but now results in a market correction. Micron's stock dropped from $471 to around $332 amid the price collapse.

To the RAM scalpers out there - why didn't you buy PMs or BTC instead?

Early replies (first 3)

62057141
The price of ram has nothing to do with the stock ponzi valuation of ram producers

62057652
out of the hundreds of ram sticks that i've had, CORSAIR is the only fucking manufacturer that had dead sticks. either DOA or dying after years of use and probably glitchy all along. fuck corsair and fuck niggers who buy anything other than 32GB or higher sticks like that shit in op's pic

62058083
>>62057652
I don't really keep up with the market anymore, I had 2 8GB corsair sticks from a set left over from half a decade ago and needed more RAM, so now there are 4 of them and they are all hitting 3600. Very happy.

I don't know what brands are good anymore. Checked current prices, scary. I remember when RAM was like $50 or something.

You are supposed to get 2 sticks of 32GB for 65GB using the dual-channel shit, ideally, right?

I don't really understand. I also don't see how modern games ran when I had 16GB total, I remember needing 64 for shit.

Much confuse, wow, I don't know what's going on anymore.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62058108
>>62057124
>RAM scalpers
Scalpers aren't a real thing, only companies selling things lower than their real market price and scalpers coming in to even things

62058331
>>62058083
32GB was the bare minimum like 10 years ago if you did anything at all meaningful on a pc

62058649
>>62058331
This is blatantly false. I don't know if RAM got faster or what, but you can run Witcher 3 / Cyberpunk 2077 on max settings with 16GB.

You can also run a shitton of Docker containers with 32GB. Not even sure when you would need 64GB today, since AI would still be on GPU.

Guessing GPU and SSD take a lot these days. I do feel like I used to need 64, blew my mind to realize my PC had 16 before I put the two additional corsairs in.

62058671
>>62058108
t. scalper

62058701
16 worked just fine until I played Rust, which crashed OOM, so I went to 32 and haven't had a problem since. Just wanted to remind everyone that it's this weird looking dude who made the memory prices spike when he bought a year and a half of the entire world's memory chip production for his data centers, which he needs to build to ask the AI how to pay the money back.

item.title: canada tax help — 11 replies — 16.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
canada tax help

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-03-31 17:26 UTC

OP

I'm so fucked /biz/

>net worth down 95% this year in shitcoins
>homeless, no IRL assets , only crypto
>realised a big paper gain on shitcoins last 2 years and I owe the government a bunch of money
>I technically have enough crypto to pay it barely, but it will pretty much wipe my remaining coins at the bottom
> the CRA starts calling me to shake me down for the money
>Grok tells me to claim that I can't pay the taxes
>tell the CRA officer that I'm homeless and can't pay
>CRA says ok, asks me to fill out a form summarizing all transactions from my bank statements for the last 3 months
> I say OK, no problem
>have like $3k in bank, haven't put real money out or into crypto in like a year

>recieve the form
>It asks me to declare my bank transactions

>but ALSO my assets and net worth

>so if I don't declare my crypto value , I would be committing fraud (?)

>CRA can't seize anything from me, because i don't own anything physical and I don't have a job (live comfy basement life with parents) but I still don't want this heat.

>will also have to declare more gains that are now zero'd in the April tax return
>paying all this tax will totally wipe my portfolio and all of my life's work

what do biz

Should I just offer to pay $2k a month or something ? I don't know what to do. i just hope the crypto market goes up

Early replies (first 3)

62055367
"Lose" your crypto. Either say it was stolen ("sent to another address without your permission") or you somehow lost access to your private keys (eg. petty theft of your bag which had your HW wallet, or accidentally threw it into trash and disposed to landfill).

THEN, after this heat is done, run a "donation campaign" asking people to help you out financially, and an "anonymous donor" donates almost exactly how much crypto you lost.

62055433
>>62055367
Can't my transactions be traced onchain?

(Note: I haven't formally declared ownership of any wallet yet. The government doesn't know yet which wallets I use but they could "theoretically" trace everything.)

62055506
how can you declare crypto assets if you never knew about them? retard

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62055529
>>62055314
Just pay the tax, you fuck. Someone needs to pay for my CCB.

62055552
taxes are voluntary
ask for an itemized bill, verified and signed as true
there is a difference between the legal person, and you, the man

62055610
>>62055314
I'm so fucked on my taxes. I have over 20,000 transactions for 2025. I have to literally go through every one and classify them as either self transfers (not taxed) or purchases (taxes). Even with tax software it's going to be a nightmare. I'll 100% be audited.

62055742
Why would you pay taxes if they don’t know? Do you have gf? Find a vietnamese gf, use her passport for binance account (vpn). Tax in vietnam is 0% right now and soon to 0.1% per txn either gain or loss. Have fun

62056155
>>62055433
Nobody is tracing that shit. I got $12k back for my tax return.

62056219
>>62055433
Yes they could. So you'd have to do it properly, theoretically speaking.
Hypothetically, something like this:
>"Lose" your crypto. Travel overseas and then, from your new temporary IP address, send your crypto to a new wallet. This is the moment you "lost" your crypto, perhaps to some petty thieves.
>Send those crypto to a mixer. This will make your crypto kinda "illicit" in several jurisdictions and CEXs probably won't want to touch them. However, you have now effectively renounced ownership of the coins. No one can prove they're yours.
>Wait for tax authorities to cool off on your current situation
>Make a video begging for donations
>Send the crypto to a new wallet which you are not afraid to declare you own. The crypto of course came from an anonymous donor who you have no idea who it is.
>Nobody can prove anything

62056233
>>62055367
I wondered how that fucker alex jones kept 10 million during his lawsuits. A "grand donor" just happened to give him a laptop the day they were over.

62058523
>>62055610
just give them your coinbase transactions and call it a day. give them more if they ask for it, but they probably wont

item.title: KABOOOOOOOOOOM **MASSIVE** RIPPLE XRP NEWS JUST LEAKED — 8 replies — 14.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
KABOOOOOOOOOOM **MASSIVE** RIPPLE XRP NEWS JUST LEAKED

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-03-31 17:33 UTC

OP

Ripple is partnering with Convera to expand enterprise cross-border payments.

Together, they’re combining global payment rails with stablecoin-enabled settlement to improve speed, liquidity, and reliability.

>https://x.com/Ripple/status/2038969816187285516 (not fake or clickbait)

Early replies (first 3)

62057459
>>62057451
So bullish

62057468
>>62057451
>Convera

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62057502
Bullish xrp

62057599
uhh can i get a

BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62057946
>>62057599
checked and BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62057993
>>62057451
>partnering
>enterprise
>cross-border payments
>global payment rails
>stablecoin-enabled settlement
>speed
>liquidity
>reliability

62058568
>>62057993
BOOOOOOOOOOOOZZWORRDSSMM!

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 221 replies — 43.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 221 • Updated: 2026-03-31 12:59 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62044561
>>62044411
That sounds really tough my fren. Siddartha Gautama said "Attachment is the root cause of all suffering.". Just practice detachment and sooner or later you'll be able to accept your loss. Maybe even grief it if it's a big loss

62044605
>>62044411
May the Vishnu on the blockchain be with you

62044783
>>62044411
Another thing Siddartha Gautama said is that the only two things you're entitled two in life is suffering and death.

62045355
>>62044561
>>62044605
>>62044783

Not very encouraging to attract new buyers

62045388
>>62045355
Ah. How so?

62045442
>>62045388
Perhaps share influential news

We know DOT is a game changer in the Web world.

62045475
>>62045442
>Perhaps share influential news
Like what?

62045958
>>62045442
100$ end of bull run. Here are some influential news for you

62048109
May Vishnu of the blockchain bless you and bless DOT to 100$.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62048431
At least 119 of 200 posts are by OP. This should violate a rule.

62048708
>>62045475
You mean there are none?
>>62048431
Obamaleaf was worse

62048850
>>62048431
>You mean there are none?
What would you regard as "influential news"?

62048939
>>62048850
What do you think? Can you define influential

62048959
>>62048939
>Can you define influential
No, because it's a subjective word. To some people a towns hall meting of a town of 500 people might be the most "influential" thing in the world since it affect them, their home town and their lives. To someone else it's a completely irrelevant event because it doesn't affect anyone or anything in their own lives. To some people the war in Iran is completely unimportant and doesn't affect them. To some people, like the Iranians is a life changing event.

The word "influential" news is in the perception of the beholder. I can give you as much stuff as you want, it's just... If you don't care about it then it's not "influental", that's why I'm asking
>What would you regard as "influential news"?
Since every individual themselves need to determine what THEY think is influential for them and tickles their funny bone.

62049007
>>62048939
Here, a google definiton; Influential describes
someone or something with the power to cause change, shape decisions, or exert significant impact on people or events. It implies having high status, authority, or being highly important (e.g., an influential leader or book).

62049042
>>62048939
>>62045442
>>62048708
If you can't even define what you want then how can I even give it to you, right? I can only give you something you can actually define.

62049654
>>62048959
>>62049007
>>62049042

You literally shill this fucking coin. Do some influencing

62051513
>>62049654
Yes. Define "influencing". What would INFLUENCE YOU? Naked ladies? Hackatons? Drugs? New technical developments? What influences YOU? If I just post something then you'll be like "No, that's not good enough", so what's the actual point if I can't give you EXACTLY what you want?

62051763
>>62051513
From reading the thread I thought you had some intellectual insight, nope an idiot

62051818
>>62051763
>From reading the thread I thought you had some intellectual insight
You tell me what you think is an "intellectual insight"? What would convince you? Throwing a hissy fit and getting emotional at me doesn't really work...

62052015
>>62051763
I'm not sure if I'm making myself clear enough. Define what you think would constitute for something that YOU consider an "intellectual insight" in your own mind? Can you post a piece of information from another chain that amazed you, that made you think, that made you say "Oh shit, this is lit"? I hope I've made myself clear enough good sar.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62056424
>>62051763
100$ end of bull run. Here, some influencing for you.


Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62056570
>>62051763
>>62049654
>>62048939
>>62048708
>>62045442
Here you go. Let me influence you young Vishnus of the mindchain; https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gg4P23PirgU
DOT will become the Web3 cloud. It'll literally be able to do work for ETH, which means it'll be able to scale ETH to millions of TPS. ETH will be able to forward transactions to DOT and DOT will be able to execute them for ETH therefore scaling ETH to millions of TPS. DOT will be able to do that for any arbitrary project (not sure about stuff like BTC and DOGE that has no smart contracts. Maybe that too, yet I can't comment on that because I really don't know at this point).

62056612
>>62056570
>DOT will become the Web3 cloud. It'll literally be able to do work for ETH, which means it'll be able to scale ETH to millions of TPS. ETH will be able to forward transactions to DOT and DOT will be able to execute them for ETH therefore scaling ETH to millions of TPS. DOT will be able to do that for any arbitrary project (not sure about stuff like BTC and DOGE that has no smart contracts. Maybe that too, yet I can't comment on that because I really don't know at this point).
This is JUST one of the possible applications. Of course you'll be able to execute ANY arbitrary operation on-chain, like any Web2 cloud can. Imagine a WHOLE GAME deployed on-chain. Literally every step of your character, every emote, every chat message, every loot drop and pick up being on-chain. You'll of course also be able to run AI agents and shit like that... DOT is the ONLY chain capable of doing MILLIONS of TPS once NOMT + JAM get implemented
NOMT alone will x10 the throughput of the whole DOT chain. https://www.rob.tech/blog/introducing-nomt/ and https://github.com/thrumdev/nomt and https://www.reddit.com/r/Polkadot/comments/1nzjhuq/what_has_happened_to_nomt/ and https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=N3BX2eTqpro

So what's getting shipped this year that's considered "influential" for out young Vishnus of the mindchain? JAM, NOMT, the Polkadot App and more stuff (instant unstaking (unstaking will go from 28 days do 1-2 days)), project individuality (proof of personhood that doesn't require KYC, 500ms block (Basti blocks), and more)

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62056623
How do I buy this shit and how long do I hold

62056631
When will you incels realize shitcoins only go up if bitcoin goes up, it doesn’t matter how much ‘bullish’ news a shitcoin gets

62056642
>>62056623
What do you mean "How do I buy this shit"? Bro, if this comes of as mean, it's not meant as mean and I'm sorry if it sounds mean. If you can't figure out how to buy something yourself then please just don't buy it at all. Make yourself a favor

>>62056631
You do realize that this won't be true forever? And that certain projects do decouple from BTC when they're ripe for it.

62056686
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gg4P23PirgU

Oliver makes a really good point here where he tries to point out that like 4 years ago L2s could just exist and people would use them and NOW people are demanding that the L2s upgrade and improve in order to still be considered useful. At this point I would like to add an additional thing. The thing that people want from major L2s (like Arbitrum and AVAX and other stuff like that) is what DOT L2s already have. The major L2s can't compete with DOT because they're behind and once JAM hits they'll be even more behind. In the time DOT gets JAM the L2s won't even have what DOT has RIGHT NOW. DOT has shared security, it has built-in native SAFE bridging (that's not even a bridge, it's a XCM, it uses HRMP channels), because of the shared security it DOES NOT fragment liquidity, you can use the functionality of ANOTHER chain on DOT (so of other DOT chains and other chains in general that aren't on DOT). Once people realize that they can EASILY build on DOT while every other chains LIMITS them in some shape or form they'll start flocking to DOT. Every other chain has a ton of problems that DOT simply DOES NOT therefore DOT is technologically speaking superior. Sooner or later the developers will catch onto it and start developing on DOT since the other chains have limited capabilities. You can only develop humdrum applications on ETH, no innovation will ever come out of ETH ever again. ETH is obselete and unreconcilable. Sure it'll stay relevant for a few more year but how much longer can it stand the test of time? BTC is windows 1.0, ETH is windows 98, DOT is Windows 12 lol (Yes, not Windows 11, it's windows 12).

62056998
>>62056631
The game is changing. This bear market will prove it

62057110
>>62056998
>The game is changing.
What do you see anon? Help us understand what you see when you say "The game is changing". And yes, I do agree that stuff will shift from stupidity back to usability. So, meme coins were the LAST DROP. Enough is ENOUGH. We need to go back to our roots and invest into stuff that actually makes sense and is GROUND BREAKING. If we don't pivot in this liminal time then the industry as a whole will be finished for like 10-20 years UNTIL we pivot there is no way forward. We need to REALIZE that BTC is crap, ETH is crap, SOL is crap, USDT is centralized shit, XRP is useless, TRX is a cult run piece of shit, lol BCH is in the top 10 WTF, DOGE is crap, BNB is fucking crap. It's all just useless junk. Literally the whole of top 10 is fucking garbage that's unusable. It's literally all Windows 1 and Windows 98 type of coins while we already have the Vishnu of the mindchain, the Windows 12 of blockchains, the collectibe 1/9 Lamborghini Veneno Roadster of the blockchain, the fucking Rolls-Royce of the blockchain industry, and we're still stuck with the fucking Windows 1 and Windows 98 of the blockchain industry. Guys, it's time for FUCKING CHANGE. Either we pivot to the 3rd generation blockchains or the whole sector will GO UNDER for a decade or two. Do you want to suck cock for 10 to 20 years? Or do you just market sell your garbage gen 1 and gen 2 tokens/coins and move on with your life and buy the actual technical innovation? It's choice time and it's GOOD to know that any day you can change your whole fucking life. If you want the world to change then the first step to changing the world is to CHANGE YOUR FUCKING SELF. You only need to 1. WANT THE CHANGE and 2. BELIEVE YOU CAN CHANGE (Self-efficacy). Those are the 2 most important principles of BEHAVIOURAL CHANGE. Change your behaviour and with that you'll change the world. Sure it'll take time and effort but hey, even wallowing in your misery, being depressed takes time and effort.

item.title: What the fuck is going on with MSFT? How is this not a great entry? Convince me to not go all in by monday. — 76 replies — 10.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
What the fuck is going on with MSFT? How is this not a great entry? Convince me to not go all in by monday.

Open thread • Replies: 76 • Updated: 2026-03-31 10:56 UTC

OP

What the fuck is going on with MSFT?
How is this not a great entry?
Convince me to not go all in by monday.

Early replies (first 3)

62043952
because the music has stopped

fucking cretin

62043962
Just buy gold and gold mining stocks.

62043971
>>62043947
I would wait until i see signs of of absolute panic.
And even then only go in for a medium term trade.
Liquidity is not great and there are big IPOs coming that will absorb even more of it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62050512
>>62043947
Is anyone even using Windows for anything other than Claude?

Windows is becoming a Claude browser. You turn on your PC, open claude, and it does everything you used to use Windows for.

Then one day anthropic is going to implement a virtual machine and a file viewer directly into Claude, so you wont ever need to use Windows again.

Its over for microsoft at this point.

62050528
>>62043947
good luck with your jeetsoft

62050538
I boughted a $500 jan/2027 leap at $400
Watch these niggers go bankrupt

62050570
I work as a Dynamics365 software developer. Do you guys think is time to switch to another career?

62050633
>>62043947
Nobody has ever gone broke betting on Microsoft. They are a shit company, but the entire world runs on Excel spreadsheets.

62050873
>>62043947
Just do it. This is a temporary economic crisis almost at the same level as covid. You can set yourself up for a great situation once the dust settles. I was a millionaire after the crash in 2020 because I never stopped my DCA and I loaded up lumps sums of cash into tech stocks and BTC while everyone here was posting pinkjaks.

62050883
>>62043947
Hey, I work at Microsoft as a Senior Data Center Technician. It's an absolute mess. We're have soooo many data centers going up, including an OpenAI project, but with the cracks clearly showing in OpenAI's profitability, as well as Sora getting shut down, there is a lot of tension in the air. We just got a hiring freeze nationwide, if I'm not mistaken. Our network engineers are all outsourced to India. We do not have an on-site NOC at any of our facilities, as far as I'm aware. These street shitter network engineers are completely incompetent and projects drag because of them. In addition, dIvERsiTy iZ oUr StrEngt here, so a lot of new techs we hire are incompetent brown/black trash. One manager just hired his son. I'm probably going to be quitting the data center world once this bubble pops, which is most likely this year.

62051391
>>62043947
Because when you go all in on AI, you lose. Shrimple as.

62051399
Great entry for a short

62051403
>>62050633
No one went broke betting on the turkey before thanksgiving

62051497
>>62043947
the company is jeeted to a point where you have to wonder if they can ever get back from that

62051499
Biz as always, low IQ people blaming everything on indians. Like if MICROSOFT hasn't been shit forever. But now, it's all indians fault. Sure buddies. Fucking retards

62051760
>>62049635
>>62049655
You're burying the lead. The level of goodwill they lost from the Xbone cannot be understated. They were saying insane shit like the kinect would be able to count how many people were in your living room watching a movie, and the rental price would dynamically change to accommodate it. And you wouldnt even be able to lend your game discs out, theyd be hard linked to your console.

Only for Sony to hit the stage at the next E3 and announce a "dynamic" new way to lend your game to friends. Only for one exec to walk up, then cheekily hand his ps4 disc to another exec. And some shit about all processing being handled in "the cloud", which gamers immediately saw through as Oh we wont really own our games theyll be on Microshit's servers forever. It was a fucking debacle all around. Even the price, Xbone was $499, PS4 was $399.

62051770
”we support bipoc trans gamers” - minecraft title screen after jeetsoft acquisition

62051787
>>62043947
In those 3 peaks I see
>exuberance
>metered enthusiasm that is beginning to wane
>desperation
where we are now, it might try to rechallenge the previous high, but it will fall short and then we'll start to see the 'exposé' articles hit the news after it starts plummeting
I have no position in ms, just how I see it going down

62051789
>>62051499

>Microsoft was always a mixed bag
>now there are Indians on top of that

how does this not make sense to you

62051794
The company is failing because it is too jeeted. The cheap labor pumped up the stock to astronomically high levels. But now the effects of that are lowering the value of the company product. So the sock has to go down as investors look to alternatives for better products.

62051822
>>62051497
Usually when the rot (Indians or other nepo groups) gets past a certain points its just irreversibly fucked.
Not a collaspe by any means but just slowly fading into irrelevance and incompetence like the legacy tech companies (IBM, HPE, whatever)

62051849
>>62051822

IBM is the best example. It was huge. America's first premier tech company. It should still be so. But nope. They were the first to take the mass Indianification poison pill, greedy execs looking for cheaper workers, until it backfired and the Indian clan had enough collective power to take it over entirely. Now it's just an eternally irrelevant dinosaur and they squat on the embers collecting whatever spare pennies they can off of it.

62051889
>>62051499
msft was garbage first, but they improved and XP and later Win7 were kinda ok, but then sudenly they started implementing jeets and everything went to shit

62051895
>>62043947
MacroHard gonna pummel Microjeet like a Pajeeta girl on her first night off the plane in Texas getting pounded by big white cock.

62052017
>>62049474
and it still isn’t worth it to use

62052128
>>62043947

It’s because they attached themselves to the hip to openAI. Same as oracle, market knows Altman is a scammer

62053062
>>62048780
Azure is also trash, even open AI is trying to switch to AWS and they opened a law suit against them.

62053066
>>62049594
>They deserve to die for what they did to Halo
This.

62054681
>>62050175
sybau

62054695
>>62043947
No Indian company should be worth 2.67T

62054705
>>62047799
Remember that firmware update from Microsoft a few months back that supposedly was a bug that fried few peoples SSDs?
Good times

62054994
>>62051399
no it isnt, you shouldve shorted months ago lol now its a long ish wait 2-3 weeks for hammer fists

62056532
>>62054695

62053849

62053854
>>62053648

Why do you think the banks lobbied so hard against the CLARITY Act allowing yield on stablecoins? They're weeing themselves at the prospective of alternatives to their scams. Just imagine how much could potentially flow into crypto once the dam breaks.

62054102
>>62053647
Shkreli is one the greatest American heroes of the 2010s.

62054117

item.title: The USA is holding a press conference about iran at 8am eastern burger time. — 39 replies — 9.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
The USA is holding a press conference about iran at 8am eastern burger time.

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-03-31 13:53 UTC

OP

The USA is holding a press conference about iran at 8am eastern burger time. We're either going to pump or dump afterwards bros. Are you derisking?

Early replies (first 3)

62056269
>>62056263
derisking is for fags
nothing
ever
happens

62056270
there is nothing I can do to weather this gay war except sit on my nuts for a couple months and make zero moves.
Thankfully I don't have any leveraged or short-term positions aside from some calls that are a very small portion of my port and could possibly recover before january

62056292
The only winners here are the insiders playing futes.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62056461
>>62056458
Fucking hell, this is is the literal basics not even trying to figure out actual "breaking news", you get 1 free spoonfeed
https://www.war.gov/News/Today-in-DOW/

62056475
>>62056263
>We're either going to pump or dump afterwards
Crapto: down only (useless asset during a global recession)
PMs: up only (the petrodollar is dead)

62056479
>>62056456
This more than likely. Orange migger allegedly told his aides that he's willing to end the war with Iran even if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed. Imagine his announcement will be something similar, but it's weapons grade horseshit and ground troops are still coming.

62056485
>>62056263
https://www.timesofisrael.com/trump-said-to-tell-aides-hes-willing-to-end-iran-war-without-reopening-hormuz/

orange nigger is going to announce we won bigly. the war, a terrific war, maybe even the greatest war ever fought, is ending with a wind down of operations in 2 more weeks. NO REFUNDS. MIGA!

62056492
>>62056485
why the heck is everyone selllling and the shorts are stacking up huge on hyperliquid

62056498
>>62056492
They want to become rocket fuel

62056506
>>62056492
They love cz.

62056538
>>62056263
Eventually Trump will declare victory and withdraw. Iran will have established future deterrence, and a hearty new revenue source of ~2M tax per tanker.

The US will be humiliated and neutered on the world stage where everyone sees this as a crushing defeat, but narcissist psychotic Trump will continue to gloat about killing an 80 year old and blowing up some bass boats he calls a Navy.

Bitcoin will pump.

62056540
I hope hegseth drops a giga TACO in 1 hour, like mission accomplished or some shit
Then I will sell everything anyway

62056541
>>62056538
The entire world has now seen how you can defeat the US.

The Iranian arc has filled me with hope. There are still good men left in this world. It doesn't matter anymore how this all ends. What matter is that a few brave men stood against many. That's what's important. Valour pleases us. Yes. And Iran has proven that there are still courageous men not willing to submit to the degeneracy of the Judeo-American empire.

62056544
>>62056475
>(useless asset during a global recession)
Are you telling me gas stations don’t accept bitcoin?

62056546
I think if you were going to sell, you would have already done it

We go up, bigly

62056550
>>62056492
>willing to end Iran war without reponeing Hormuz
>We did it, Patrick, we saved the city!
>W-why is everyone still selling

I realize the market is high on cope fumes, but declaring yourself the winner doesn't automatically make it true

62056573
>>62056541
DEATH TO SATAN INTENSIFIES!

62056789
its about to start niggas

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CF7Th9PnPsw

62056797
Just expect everything to dump, the USD is devaluing day by day and nothing is realistically affordable.

62056814
Is it right now or in one hour?

62056824
nothingburger.

62056831
Get to the point, asswipe

62056839
>We are winning so hard right now trust me

62056843
tldw the war is over its safe to buy now bros

62056852
>>62056789
Bro wtf, I tuned in and they are just restreaming a private pre-recorded youtube? It even glitched on stream.

62056872
>>62056852

just watch it here

62056875
I hope he says we are pulling out and Europe can just deal with no oil lmao

62056881
>>62056875
nothing happened, he just said we're winning, they're losing and they better make a deal because Trump doesn't bluff.

62056909
So,nothingburger?

62056936
>>62056909

62057304

62057350
The scheduled taco is on Friday. Relax anon

item.title: Idiot — 12 replies — 7.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Idiot

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-03-31 16:30 UTC

OP

I opened a BTC trade at 105k, ask me anything

Early replies (first 3)

62057597
>>62057584
are u ok?

62057606
>>62057597
No.

62057621
>>62057606
:(

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62057676
>>62057584
this is a slurping year. you should average down. and ride it back up. you'll need 5 BTC to retire by 2035 so don't waste this opportunity. you are still buying, right?

62057686
>>62057676
If you're betting on the next 10 years, why buy now? Why not wait until October when it will be $40-50k?

62057805
>>62057686
Because le heckin accurate 4 year cycle is known to everyone by now so we might not see it dip that low. We are already below the previous ATH which is an anomaly by itself.

62057900
>>62057686
>why not time the bottom?
it never feels like the bottom at the bottom so i just buy on a schedule stress free.

62057917
>>62057597
BUSTAH WOLF

62057920
>>62057900
Buying on a schedule isn't much better than trading based on gut feeling of what "feels" like the bottom.

62057969
>>62057920
you must have a trustworthy gut. good for you.
the closer we get to the 200w ma the more i buy.

it works for me to subtract emotion and just make it a numbers game. i don't buy when the price is extended 25% over the 200w ma.

62058042
>>62057969
Track liquidity maps and chain flows. You'll know the bottom is in when the sellers have run out of stock.
Or just ask chatgpt or whatever to do it for you. It'll probably lag behind by an hour or so, but they're pretty capable of giving a decent summary.

62058162
>>62057584
how often does your lender visit and is lube an option?

item.title: ½ Trillion Gone — 90 replies — 6.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
½ Trillion Gone

Open thread • Replies: 90 • Updated: 2026-03-31 04:53 UTC

OP

The US stock market wiped out $500 Billion dollars today. You did sell all your goyboxes, didn't you anon?

Early replies (first 3)

62036869
Haha, just kidding. It was actually a $1 Trillion dollars.

62036893
i bought the dip
panicans lose
TTP

62036894
unless you're a day trader none of this matters and even if you're a day trader still none of this matters you will lose your money regardless. fud doesn't work on proper investment strategies. longing the market won game over bye bye pink wojaks farming crypto queer

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62042954
>>62036869
WHAT THE FUCK 1,000,0000,00000 TRILLONS YOU MEAN? HOW THE FUCK THE FED PRINTED SO MANY FAKE MONEY?? LMFAO

62043042
>>62036869
woooooooow so many zeroes

62043961
>>62036853
I voted for this

62045345
>>62037625
money is a measurement of value, and you can't print value. all this spending and inflation has yet come home to roost

62046233
>>62043042
how much is 1 trillion trillion

62046276
>>62037465
it's funny how ukrainians have such an inflated ego. They kidnap people from the streets to send them to war and they think they can teach america about war? kek

62046618
>>62037465
Our military spending is nothing.
Only 15 cents on every dollar you pay in taxes goes to it.
15 more cents goes to paying interest because our boomer overlords couldn’t balance the budget.
70 cents literally just goes to boomers, through SS, medicaid, etc. (Though there are a few fraudulent outliers like those Somalian daycares.)

Meanwhile in china
Percentages of government spending.
Social Security & Employment: ~15.4%
Education: ~15.1%
Agriculture, Forestry & Water: ~8.2%
Health and Family Planning: ~7.4%
Urban & Rural Community Affairs: ~7.2%
General Public Service: ~7.2%
Science & Technology: ~4.2%
Transportation: ~4.2%
National Defense: ~5.9% (Probably under reported tho)
Interest Payments on Debt: ~4.7%

The fucking Chicom boomers could run our country better than the neoliberal and neoconservative boomers have.

62046627
>>62046618
I will counterpoint that a lot of the stuff on the chinese budget is just jobs programs bullshit make-work.
But it’s still somehow better than the current US setup of 70% (!) retirement home money and the rest is just MIC grift and interest.

62046644
>>62037625
>Just print more money

62046806
>>62037100
When Iran decides its over. They control the Hormuz straight (and soon, the Red Sea), America can't do shit about it, and they aren't in the mood to negotiate because A: They're winning and B: Orange nigger and his mossad buddies use negotiations as an opportunity for assassination perfidy

62047109
>>62036969
you can see where raegan 5x'd the national debt

62047195
>>62046618
You're not seeing the full picture. 60% of industry in China is state-owned, hence industrial spending is represented in government expenditure. But it's the same in United States - the vast majority of "state spending" occurs via the issuance and rehypothecation of public and private credit, enabled by the central bank, which is only nominally independent. The US larps as a free economy when actively it is controlled by banks and corporate interests that tell you that social security is the problem and not the trillions going into their pockets.

62047266
>>62036853
stocks are a bubble and a scam, you don't own anything if you have stocks and you don't finance anything in the economy

62050467
>>62047195
That’s a fair point but banking exists in both systems and they’re different to what i‘m talking about.
The banks, even the state run ones in china, operate on the basis that the money you put in will at least still be there the day after tomorrow.
Taxes have no such basis, you pay them and they’re gone, they aren’t coming back.

But China’s government (tax budget) spending is still technically ~30% of GDP like the US’s.
If the governments are taking the same amount anyways, I would take the chink bridges-to-nowhere tax budget ANYYY day over the USA’s 70% spend on old people, and ““disability”” grifters.

62052384
1.2 TRILLION GONE?????

62052753
>>62036853
Copilot told me to hold my MULL after the earnings call and when the current phase of the Iran war started. Lost so much money, it almost wiped out my previous month's earnings as well, erasing all my milestones. It took a thousand cuts out of nowhere, but I learned about leveraged ETF decay. I thought Copilot helped me, but it turns out telling people to hold is generic advice and just so happened to work initially.

62053333
>>62036908
>remind me in two weeks

62053419
>>62052753
Imagine listening let alone taking the advice of chatbot. Financial advice even. Its just going to pull the top google search result and then shit it out to you in a reddit cool wine aunt looking for updoots format.

62053702
>>62037040
>absolute retard still doing technical analysis in the big 26

62053730
>>62036906
Oh no! Not my savings that I won't cash out for another three decades and by that point this "crash" will barely register as a small blip!

62053780
>>62036908
That's some serious cope, brah.

62053800
>>62046618
>70 cents literally just goes to boomers
>60 cents just to non-whites

62053846
>>62037465
unironically waste is often a direct response to wealth. Budget constraints and resource scarcity force you to come up with interesting and innovative solutions to problems, using as few resources as possible.
When you have what appears to be infinite money, you don't have to be careful with your budget. Lose a radar system worth a billion dollars? Lockheed Martin will make a new one for you lickety-split. See an enemy aircraft? Fire a full salvo of patriot missiles at it, and make sure it comes down. Why not? You've got thousands more in the warehouse.
That's also the reason why they left all that military hardware for the dunecoons to take. It'd be inconvenient to transport and/or store them, and the US can just write a blank check to the military industrial complex to print a thousand more shitty armored humvees for immediately use.

62053912
>>62046618
last time I checked, we spent around 40 billion on energy, and 35 billion of that went to maintaining nuclear weapons.

62053986
>>62036853
>>62036869
>1,000,000,000,000 Trillion

Anyways, I'm tired of explaining economics to you fools. Nothing was "wiped out".

62054651
>>62037465
Even if it ends tomorrow, it's not going to go back to how it was before.
Lots of stuff is permanently destroyed, it takes a year just to clean it up, and up to a decade to build it back.
People really don't know how bad it's going to become and how long it's going to last and how it's becoming worse with every single day.
I'm looking forward for all the billions of starving jeets, niggers, spics, chinks.

62054741
>>62036869
>Haha, just kidding. It was actually a $1 Trillion virtual digits in a computer
>in a casino that don't exist.
They aren't dollars until you sell your digital boomer casino digits and convert them to dollars.

62055475
>>62053846
The resource you think you have infinite of is often the one you’re most likely to run out of first.
You apply that to a group of people and it’s 100x more likely.
This is a known phenomenon in psychology.

62055766
>>62037465
>it could be too late
has been for a while. we have $200 trillion in unfunded medicare and social security obligations. it's not politically possible to cut spending or raise taxes. so all we can do is borrow until we collapse
>>62054651
optimistic of you to think that it will be possible to rebuild, ever

62055786
>>62036853
That actually is a big blow to capitalism. We are winning reddit!
